top_categories.workout_context: GFNY Kuala Lumpur's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
Race-pace efforts that mimic GFNY Kuala Lumpur's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
At mile 60 of 86, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of GFNY Kuala Lumpur. Train it until it feels boring.
GFNY Kuala Lumpur's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 86 miles.
At GFNY Kuala Lumpur, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
2-minute all-out efforts on mixed terrain. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 86 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what GFNY Kuala Lumpur's mixed terrain actually does to you.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At GFNY Kuala Lumpur, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 86 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for GFNY Kuala Lumpur’s 86 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on TT Threshold, Race Simulation, and VO2max to prepare you for 86 miles of mixed terrain in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia.
